section.title: Feature-Level Specification
section.description: - For every feature listed in Section 6.1, provide a detailed design using the same Fx ID and feature name. - Each subsection must match the feature ID, name, and priority defined in Section 6.1. - No additional features beyond those in Section 6.1 may be specified here.

template.title: Feature Template
template.header: Feature ID: Fx | Priority: Must-Have / Should-Have / Nice-to-Have

subsection.title: User Story
subsection.description: - Describe the user scenario for implementing this feature. - Use this format: As a [persona], I want to [action], so that [benefit].
subsection.example: - As a new user, I want to sign up with my email, so that I can access the service.

subsection.title: User Flow
subsection.description: - Describe the user flow for this feature. - Keep the flow simple and concise.
subsection.example: 1. Display a sign-up form with: - Email input field - Password input field - "Sign Up" button 2. When the user submits the form: - Validate input - Trigger an API call - Redirect to the main screen upon successful sign-up

subsection.title: Technical Description
subsection.description: - Describe the implementation as a vertical slice: trace each user action end-to-end from UI through API to data store, so this feature can be developed and tested independently. - For each key action in the user flow, describe what happens at each layer it touches (e.g., UI component, API endpoint, database operation). - If this feature shares resources with other features (e.g., a common table or auth middleware), note the shared interface rather than redefining it.
subsection.example: 1. Submit Sign-up Form - UI: Sign-up form component sends POST /api/auth/signup with email and password - API: /api/auth/signup endpoint validates email format, checks for duplicates, hashes password with bcrypt - Data: Insert new row into `users` table (id, email, password_hash, created_at) - Response: Return JWT token → UI stores token and redirects to main screen 2. Validate Email (real-time) - UI: On blur, send GET /api/auth/check-email?email={email} - API: Query `users` table for existing email - Response: Return availability status → UI shows inline validation message

subsection.title: Edge Cases
subsection.description: List potential edge cases and how they should be handled.
subsection.example: - User submits an already registered email → Show "Email already exists" error - User enters a password with less than 8 characters → Show validation error - Network timeout during API call → Show retry option

subsection.title: Error Handling
subsection.description: Define error scenarios and appropriate responses.
subsection.example: | Error Type | HTTP Code | User Message | |------------|-----------|--------------| | Validation failure | 400 | "Please check your input" | | Duplicate email | 409 | "Email already registered" | | Server error | 500 | "Something went wrong. Please try again." |

subsection.title: Acceptance Criteria
subsection.description: - Define the conditions that must be met for this feature to be considered complete. - Use clear, testable statements.
subsection.example: - [ ] User can enter email and password in the sign-up form - [ ] Form validates email format before submission - [ ] Password must be at least 8 characters with one special character - [ ] Successful sign-up redirects user to main screen - [ ] Duplicate email shows appropriate error message - [ ] All error states display user-friendly messages
